channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Air Canada pilot accused of flying for 17 years without proper licence
item.description: Former airline captain charged with fraud after allegedly commanding more than 900 flights without required credentials.

item.title: Bolivia approves military measures against nationwide protests
item.description: Bolivia approves military measures against nationwide protests.

item.title: Iran attacks Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan in retaliation for US strikes
item.description: Strikes come after US attacked Iranian ports and islands in the Strait of Hormuz over the downing of a helicopter.

item.title: Protesters torch cars, buildings in Belfast after refugee knife attack
item.description: Unrest comes after a Sudanese man was arrested over a stabbing attack in north Belfast, UK.

item.title: Belfast plunged into chaos as vehicles set ablaze following stabbing attack
item.description: Al Jazeera's Milena Veselinovic reports from Belfast, where protests have escalated into violent riots.

item.title: South Korea’s booming stock market mints generation of novice investors
item.description: South Korean stocks are soaring, drawing first-time investors in a nation where property has long reigned supreme.

item.title: Brazil intercepts 108 Cuban immigrants amid growing asylum applications
item.description: Last year, for the first time in a decade, Cuban asylum applications exceeded Venezuelan ones in sign of growing strain.

item.title: Iran war live: Tehran targets US forces in Bahrain, Jordan after south hit
item.description: Lebanon's Health Ministry says the death toll from Israeli attacks on the country since March 2 has reached 3,666.

item.title: US military launches ‘self-defence strikes’ targeting Iran
item.description: US Central Command (CENTCOM) says its forces began launching ‘self-defence’ strikes against Iran.

item.title: Afghan authorities open fire on protesters over women’s dress code
item.description: Authorities suppressed a protest in western Afghanistan over the detention of women for dress code violations.

item.title: US judge halts execution by nitrogen gas, ruling it unconstitutional
item.description: Judge Emily Marks had previously allowed the execution to proceed, arguing that no execution is entirely without pain.

item.title: Mississippi residents sue Musk’s xAI and SpaceX over data centre ‘nuisance’
item.description: Three residents filed the case on behalf of a class estimated at more than 10,000 members.

item.title: Deadly protests in Pakistan-administered Kashmir: What’s going on?
item.description: At least 11 people were killed on Sunday in clashes between police and protesters in Pakistan-administered Kashmir.

item.title: Trump vows retaliation after claiming Iran shot down Apache helicopter
item.description: President Trump accused Iran of shooting down a US Apache helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz

item.title: Israeli forces kill nine in Tyre after issuing forced displacement orders
item.description: Israeli forces killed nine people in Tyre in the past 24 hours after it issued forced displacement orders on the city.

item.title: Canada confirms opening of Gordie Howe Bridge, despite Trump’s threats
item.description: Trump had threatened to halt the opening of the cross-border bridge, which is jointly owned by Michigan and Canada.

item.title: Bear captured after days of panic and school closures in Japan
item.description: A bear that triggered days of disruption in the Japanese city of Utsunomiya was captured.

item.title: Iran conflict: Why has oil stayed near $100 a barrel?
item.description: The worst-case oil scenario has been avoided, but inflation and slower growth continue to weigh on the global economy.

item.title: What will the fallout be from the unrest in Pakistan-administered Kashmir?
item.description: Recent clashes between protesters and police killed at least 11 people.

item.title: Why so many Americans haven’t heard about the USS Liberty
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Ava Warriner explains what happened on the day of the attack, the alleged cover-up that followed.

item.title: NASA announces astronauts for Artemis III spaceflight, scheduled for 2027
item.description: Questions linger over whether the explosion of the Blue Origin New Glenn rocket will affect the Artemis III mission.

item.title: Israel preventing more than 16,500 Palestinians from accessing medical treatment
item.description: Israel has systematically destroyed Gaza's healthcare system since launching its genocidal war in October 2023.

item.title: ‘Anything but normal’: Uncertainty as Iranians try to get by amid US war
item.description: War damage and blockade have worsened economic conditions for many struggling Iranians.

item.title: Palestinians confront Israeli settlers over land grab near Hebron
item.description: Palestinians confronted Israeli settlers near Hebron over their attempt to seize Palestinian land

item.title: Mexico prepares for World Cup opening match amid protests
item.description: Final preparations are underway for the opening of the 2026 FIFA World Cup at Mexico City's Azteca Stadium.
